evidence: topsoil soil organic carbon (SOC) in native savanna plots increased linearly with mean annual precipitation (MAP) between 900 and 1700 mm, after controlling for temperature and clay content.
Higher MAP drives greater above-ground biomass input
Higher MAP drives greater above-ground biomass input and slows decomposition through seasonal moisture regulation.
time since parent-material exposure and temperature jointly govern the rate of primary-mineral dissolution.
time since parent-material exposure and temperature jointly govern the rate of primary-mineral dissolution.
clay content at the Bt horizon is controlled primarily by parent material: gneiss-derived soils accumulated 45-60% clay by mass, whereas sandstone-derived profiles stabilised at 20-30%.
The resulting argillic horizon in turn controls effective Cation Exchange Capacity.
TWI is positively associated with surface-layer soil moisture
TWI... negatively associated with bulk density
higher TWI promotes organic-matter retention by slowing aerobic decomposition, so TWI is a proxy covariate for SOC in this landscape.

Conversion of native Cerrado vegetation to continuous pasture reduced topsoil SOC stocks by 21-38% within 15 years, even without concomitant erosion. | Land use therefore affects SOC through altered root inputs.
The dominant mechanism is reduced below-ground biomass input under shallow-rooted pasture grasses relative to the deep-rooted native savanna.
Hence slope drives erosion
and erosion drives SOC loss
Fire frequency was the single largest driver of SOC differences across protected and unmanaged savanna plots, after controlling for MAP and clay content.
Soil clay content was the primary determinant of plant-available water capacity across 240 Cerrado Oxisol and Latosol profiles.
Higher clay content increased water retention in the 0-40 cm layer
Higher clay content increased water retention in the 0-40 cm layer, and this in turn raised maize grain yield under rainfed management, particularly during late-season dry spells.
Any regression of SOC on NDVI must adjust for MAP, slope and TWI as common ancestors.
Precipitation and topographic position jointly drive vegetation greenness; NDVI is therefore a useful synthesis covariate, but not a direct cause of SOC.
Low pH reduces phosphorus availability
Low pH reduces phosphorus availability and inhibits root development.
Liming raises pH
Liming raises pH and increases both exchangeable Ca and available P
Liming raises pH and increases both exchangeable Ca and available P
Parent-material weathering and leaching jointly control baseline pH.
heterotrophic CO2 efflux followed a Q10 near 2.1 for temperatures between 18 and 32 degrees Celsius.
Higher soil temperature accelerates microbial decomposition of labile SOC, shortening carbon residence time.
Higher soil temperature accelerates microbial decomposition of labile SOC, shortening carbon residence time.
On hillslopes, south-facing microclimates therefore accumulate more SOC than north-facing slopes at similar altitude.
